daily_review.narrative:
## Solid Night Despite One Blowup

Finished 5-4 with a +6.1% ROI, carried by the low-confidence tier while medium plays gave back some profit.

## MEDIUM CONFIDENCE (1-2, -36.4% ROI)

The tier that should be our bread and butter had a rough night. Milwaukee-LAC Over 223 was the lone winner — both teams came in with strong offensive efficiency numbers and the Bucks on a back-to-back, and the game sailed over to 240.

Indiana-Miami was the disaster. We projected 240.6 in a game featuring two teams playing at identical elite pace (104.6 possessions). The actual total hit 253, a 12.4-point miss. Looking at the setup, Miami's defense had been leaky (118.1 points allowed per 100 possessions) and Indiana's offense was struggling (106.4), but something broke open that the rolling averages didn't capture. Both teams likely shot better than their recent form, or Miami's defensive issues were even worse than the data suggested. This one stings because the pace read was perfect — we just underestimated how many points would be scored per possession.

Toronto-Orlando Under 225 was a push-equivalent loss by a single point. Normal variance.

## LOW CONFIDENCE (4-2, +27.3% ROI)

The bargain bin delivered, going 4-2 and posting the night's best ROI. These lower-conviction plays caught some favorable variance.

## WHAT TO WATCH

That Miami defensive number (118.1 points allowed per 100 possessions) might be understating how vulnerable they are right now. When a team's defense is that porous and they're playing at elite pace, there's explosion risk on totals even when the opponent's offense looks mediocre on paper. Worth monitoring whether our defensive efficiency inputs are lagging real-time deterioration for teams in freefall.
